channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Cal blows 25-point lead to lose to No. 8 Miami after festive day in Berkeley
item.description: The Bears outscored the Hurricanes 35-10 in the first 40 minutes of the game, then it all fell apart.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Faraudo, The Mercury News

item.title: Bay FC’s big, late collapse leads to 5-1 loss to NJ/NY Gotham FC
item.description: Gotham FC turns a late 1-1 tie into a rout, scoring three goals in a rough four-minute stretch for Bay FC.
item.dc:creator: Jon Becker, The Mercury News

item.title: Washington pulls away in 4th quarter to beat No. 10 Michigan 27-17 in title game rematch
item.description: Will Rogers threw for 271 yards and a pair of first-half touchdowns, Jonah Coleman’s 1-yard TD with 6:22 left gave Washington the lead, and the Huskies beat No. 10 Michigan 27-17 in a rematch of last season’s College Football Playoff championship game.
item.dc:creator: Tim Booth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mets starter Kodai Senga goes 2 innings and passes baton to brilliant bullpen in Game 1 NLDS win
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Kodai Senga slapped the dugout railing in a raw display of emotion, a most unexpected Game 1 starter pumping his fist during an eighth-inning comeback that — have you followed these New York Mets? — should really have been expected by now.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri coach Eliah Drinkwitz addresses upset loss to Texas A&M: 'I apologize to our fans'
item.description: Missouri's College Football Playoff aspirations suffered a significant setback Saturday after the Tigers were routed by the Texas A&M Aggies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Big Sho: Ohtani hits tying 3-run homer in playoff debut for the Los Angeles Dodgers in NLDS win
item.description: Shohei Ohtani hit a tying, three-run homer in the second inning of his highly anticipated playoff debut to help the Los Angeles Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres 7-5 in their National League Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ESPN analyst wants anonymous voter who chose Angel Reese for Rookie of the Year to go public: 'Stand on it'
item.description: Only one voter decided Caitlin Clark was not the best rookie in the WNBA this season. An ESPN analyst wants that lone voter to be revealed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers' Kershaw, Guardians' Lively, Tigers' Mize and Yankees' Stroman are left off ALDS rosters
item.description: Pitchers Clayton Kershaw and Joe Kelly were left off the Los Angeles Dodgers' roster for their NL Division Series against the San Diego Padres that started Saturday. San Diego added right-handers Martín Pérez and Alek Jacob, who were skipped from the Wild Card Series roster against Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Greg Landry, Lions great and former NFL coach, dead at 77
item.description: Former Detroit Lions quarterback Greg Landry, who later returned as an assistant coach, has died at the age of 77, the team announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High school football player spanks opponents with belt after 77-0 beatdown
item.description: A high school football player in the Houston area spanked his opponents with a belt after beating them 77-0, as part of a new social media trend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Packers suspend WR Romeo Doubs vs. Rams for conduct detrimental to team
item.description: GREEN BAY, Wis. (AP) — The Green Bay Packers have suspended wide receiver Romeo Doubs from Sunday’s game with the Los Angeles Rams for what they described as conduct detrimental to the team. Doubs hadn’t practiced Thursday or Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gauff rallies to reach China Open final and Sinner, Alcaraz and Djokovic win in Shanghai
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Coco Gauff rallied from a set down for the third consecutive match as she beat Paula Badosa 4-6, 6-4, 6-2 to reach the China Open final on Saturday. The sixth-ranked Gauff will play Karolina Muchova of the Czech Republic in Sunday's final.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Odell Beckham Jr. will make his Dolphins debut vs. Patriots after being activated from PUP
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — The Miami Dolphins activated Odell Beckham Jr. from the physically unable to perform list on Saturday, clearing the way for the veteran receiver to make his season debut Sunday at New England.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cal fans break through ESPN's 'College GameDay' barricades ahead of Miami showdown
item.description: The anticipation of Cal's first ACC home game and the arrival of ESPN’s "College GameDay" crew was too much for some fans to handle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's where SoFi ranks among NFL's best (and worst) stadiums
item.description: SoFi Stadium in Inglewood is not the best stadium in the NFL but it did crack the top five, according to a survey released Thursday. Lumen Field, the home of the Seattle Seahawks, was named the best stadium for football fans to visit in USA Today's rankings of all NFL stadiums.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake plays Minnesota to scoreless draw
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After scoring eight goals in its last three games, Real Salt Lake was unable to find the back of the net in a scoreless draw with Minnesota Wednesday night at America First Field.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: From food to murder, Aaron Rodgers talks all things Berkeley ahead of College GameDay appearance
item.description: (KRON) -- The college football world will have its eyes on Berkeley this weekend as Cal hosts ESPN's famous College GameDay show for the first time before its game against No. 8 Miami on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: RSL looks to solidify playoff seeding against Minnesota
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake has already clinched an MLS playoff spot, but considering RSL is 6-1-2 all-time at home in the postseason, they want that home field advantage in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Reports: Pete Rose, baseball's all-time hits leader, dead at 83
item.description: LAS VEGAS — Pete Rose, one of Major League Baseball's most polarizing athletes and the game's all-time hits leader, has died at the age of 83, according to TMZ. Rose's agent, Ryan Fiterman, confirmed the news to TMZ and said, "the family is asking for privacy at this time.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, Fox 5

item.title: Americans get biggest road win and capture the Presidents Cup for the 10th straight time
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — U.S. captain Jim Furyk raised the gold trophy before a team that dressed in red shirts that filled the scoreboard early with red scores. The Presidents Cup is one trophy the Americans own, for two decades and counting.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: College GameDay makes first-ever appearance at Berkeley
item.description: (KRON) -- Since taking its show on the road back in 1993, "College GameDay" has been a huge presence in the college football community by being known to travel across dozens of campuses across the country.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4
